Home > Blog > Engineering > Scaling Our Infrastructure

How We Scaled Our Infrastructure to Handle 10 Million Requests Per Second

By the Engineering Team · January 15, 2024

When we hit our first million requests per second, we thought we had it figured out. We were wrong. Here's the story of scaling from 1M to 10M RPS.

The key insight was that our bottleneck wasn't CPU or memory — it was coordination. Every request that required cross-service communication added latency that compounded under load.

By redesigning our service mesh to use event-driven communication and aggressive caching at the edge, we reduced inter-service calls by 80% and achieved our 10M RPS target.
